seoTitle: How to Create a Template in Brevo Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to build a reusable email template in Brevo, from responsive layout blocks to global styling and dynamic contact variables.
intro: Building a consistent brand identity across every email starts with a solid template you can reuse instead of designing from scratch each time. This guide covers creating a new template in Brevo's visual editor, arranging responsive layout blocks, setting global fonts and colors, and adding personalization variables. It's aimed at anyone who wants a repeatable, professional design framework.

steps.heading: Start a new template
steps.body: Go to Campaigns, then Templates, and click New template to open the creation form.

steps.heading: Name and describe your template
steps.body: Enter an internal description name for tracking purposes and a subject line, then add optional preview text before clicking Next step.

steps.heading: Build the layout structure
steps.body: Use the left elements drawer to drag header spaces, text blocks, and hero banner cards onto your canvas.

steps.heading: Set global styles
steps.body: Configure master color themes, hover effects, and consistent font settings inside the visual styling sidebar.

steps.heading: Add dynamic elements and save
steps.body: Insert personalization placeholders and the unsubscribe link, test the layout across screen sizes, then save and quit to lock the template into your library.

faq.question: Can I reuse a Brevo template across multiple campaigns?
faq.answer: Yes, saved templates live in your library and can be pulled into any future campaign without rebuilding the layout.

faq.question: How do I make sure my template looks good on mobile?
faq.answer: Use the real-time screen simulator in the template editor to preview how your layout renders before locking it down.

faq.question: What are dynamic components in a template?
faq.answer: These are flexible attribute placeholders, like a contact's first name, along with the required unsubscribe link, that personalize the email automatically.

faq.question: Do I need design experience to build a template?
faq.answer: No, the drag-and-drop elements drawer lets you assemble a professional layout without any coding or design background.
